exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (V bar)
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight. Stand facing the machine, feet about hip-width apart.
Grip and posture: Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip. Step slightly back so the cable is taut, hinge very slightly at the hips, chest up, shoulders back, and eyes forward.
Starting position: Tuck your elbows close to your sides and bend the elbows so the V-bar is near chest/upper abdomen level. Keep your upper arms stationary and vertical throughout the movement.

Downward movement (concentric): Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the triceps are contracted. Keep the wrists neutral and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.
End position: Pause briefly at full extension without locking the elbows aggressively, maintaining tension on the triceps.

Return (eccentric): Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows, controlling the weight to avoid momentum. Stop when the forearms reach roughly parallel to the floor or the bar returns to chest/upper abdomen level.

Technique tips: Keep the torso stable and avoid using body swing or stepping to generate force. If you feel the delts or upper back taking over, reduce weight and re-establish tight elbows at your sides.
Repetitions and safety: Perform 8–15 controlled reps per set depending on goal; choose a weight that allows strict form. Stop if you feel sharp pain in the elbow or wrist.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using a V-bar to extend the elbows and isolate the triceps; performed from a standing position with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Emphasize a full elbow extension and controlled return to work the long and lateral heads of the triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar on the angled (inner) hand positions so your palms are facing upward/neutral; hands about shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and core braced to stabilize the spine; elbows should sit close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing your elbows, driving the movement with your biceps; keep your upper arms stationary and forearms moving.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and biceps are fully contracted; avoid shrugging or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to full elbow extension without locking the joints.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and strict form throughout.
exercises.description: Standing curl using an EZ bar with a semi-supinated grip to isolate elbow flexion and develop the biceps. Perform controlled concentric and eccentric reps while keeping the torso still to minimize cheating.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (upright torso).
Grab the SZ bar with a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing down or slightly toward you), hands about shoulder-width apart.
Start with the bar at chest/upper-ab level, elbows tucked close to your sides and upper arms stationary (forearms up).
Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and triceps are contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging or leaning forward.
Pause briefly at full extension, then inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows while maintaining upper-arm position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping movement controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar that emphasizes elbow extension to work the triceps; performed facing the cable machine with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Keep a neutral spine and controlled tempo, extending the arms fully and returning under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

exercises.name: Cable Rope High Pulley Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Set a rope attachment on a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a rope attachment on a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand with your back toward the machine, take a staggered (split) stance for stability, and lean slightly forward at the hips so your torso is angled down but spine remains neutral.
Reach overhead and grasp the rope with both hands in a neutral (thumbs-up) grip, bringing the rope ends beside your head so your upper arms are vertical or slightly forward and elbows pointing up.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms stationary (elbows fixed close to head) throughout the movement.
Exhale and extend your forearms by straightening the elbows, pulling the rope forward/overhead until your arms are fully extended but not aggressively locked out.
Pause briefly at full extension to contract the triceps.
Inhale and slowly allow the rope to return to the start position by bending the elbows, controlling the descent until your forearms are back beside or slightly behind your head.
Repeat for the desired reps while maintaining a neutral wrist position, minimal trunk movement, and steady controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Standing cable rope overhead triceps extension performed from a high pulley; you extend the elbows to work the triceps while the torso is braced in a slight forward lean. The rope allows a neutral (thumbs-up) grip and greater range of motion behind the head.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cross Body Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, core braced and shoulders relaxed.
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, core braced and shoulders relaxed.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip, arm fully extended at your side and wrist neutral; the other hand can rest lightly on your hip.
Keep your upper arm and elbow close to your torso and stationary; do not let the elbow travel forward excessively.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell up across your body toward the opposite shoulder in a diagonal path, leading with the thumb while maintaining the neutral grip.
Pause and squeeze at the top for a moment, keeping the wrist straight and the torso stable (do not lean or swing the body).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with control, fully extending the elbow but avoiding locking it harshly.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch sides and perform the same steps with the opposite arm.
Maintain steady breathing and a controlled tempo throughout; reduce weight if you must swing or use momentum.
exercises.description: A standing hammer-grip curl where the dumbbell is lifted diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder, emphasizing the biceps with strong forearm involvement. Performed with a neutral (hammer) grip to target the brachialis/brachioradialis region while minimizing wrist rotation.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Drag Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged (standing position).
Grasp an EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and let the bar hang against your thighs.
Start with your elbows slightly behind the torso and the bar touching or very close to the upper thighs.
Keeping the bar close to your body, drive your elbows backward (toward your hips) and pull the bar straight up along your torso — do not flare the elbows forward.
Curl the bar until it reaches around chest/upper abdomen level and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the bar back down along the same path until your arms are fully extended and the bar returns to the thighs, maintaining elbow position behind the body.
Breathe out while curling up and inhale while lowering; avoid swinging the torso or using momentum and keep movement controlled.
Perform desired reps while keeping wrists neutral and ensuring the bar remains in contact with or very close to the body throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing EZ-bar drag curl where the bar is pulled up close to the body while the elbows travel back, emphasizing the biceps peak and reducing shoulder involvement compared to a standard curl.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Single Arm Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core engaged to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core engaged to maintain a neutral spine.
Pick up a dumbbell with one hand and press it overhead until your arm is fully extended; keep the wrist neutral and elbow pointing forward/up.
Optionally place your free hand on the working upper arm or elbow for additional support and to help keep the upper arm vertical.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell by flexing the elbow, bringing the weight down behind your head until your forearm is roughly parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Keep the upper arm stationary and avoid excessive leaning or flaring of the elbow; only the forearm should move.
Exhale and press the dumbbell back up by extending the elbow until the arm is straight but not locked out aggressively.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Use a controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or excessive trunk movement.
exercises.description: Seated single-arm overhead triceps extension isolates the triceps by extending the elbow with a dumbbell held overhead while the upper arm remains stationary. It is performed one arm at a time to address unilateral strength and imbalance.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (upright torso).
Grab the SZ bar with a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing down or slightly toward you), hands about shoulder-width apart.
Start with the bar at chest/upper-ab level, elbows tucked close to your sides and upper arms stationary (forearms up).
Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and triceps are contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging or leaning forward.
Pause briefly at full extension, then inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows while maintaining upper-arm position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping movement controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar that emphasizes elbow extension to work the triceps; performed facing the cable machine with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Keep a neutral spine and controlled tempo, extending the arms fully and returning under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

exercises.name: Cable High Pulley Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Set a rope or handle on a high pulley.
instructions: Set a rope or handle on a high pulley. Stand facing away from the machine with feet in a staggered stance (one foot forward, one back) and grip the rope with both hands overhead.
Lean forward slightly at the hips, brace your core (abs engaged), and keep your upper arms fixed close to your head so the elbows point forward/up throughout the set.
Begin with your forearms bent so the rope is behind your head; this is the start position. Keep your delts and torso stable — only the elbows should move.
Exhale and extend your elbows, pressing the rope forward and up until your arms are nearly straight while maintaining a small, controlled bend in the elbows at lockout.
Pause briefly at full extension to maximize triceps contraction, then inhale and slowly allow the rope to return to the start position behind your head under control.
Repeat for the desired reps. Maintain neutral wrist alignment, avoid flaring the elbows outward, and keep the core tight to prevent arching the lower back.
exercises.description: An overhead triceps extension performed with a high pulley and rope attachment to load the long head of the triceps; executed from a slightly forward-leaning, staggered stance to increase stability. The movement isolates elbow extension while the torso and shoulders stabilize the load.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
instructions: Sit on a preacher (angled) curl bench with feet flat on the floor and chest/abdomen lightly against the pad so your upper arms rest flat on the sloped pad; body should be stable and upright.
Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above the pad; this is the start position (elbows slightly bent but not locked).

Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing at the elbows, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder movement; focus on contracting the biceps as the bar approaches chest level.
Pause and squeeze the biceps briefly at the top of the movement without lifting the elbows off the pad or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are near full extension, maintaining contact between upper arms and pad throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows strict form; avoid using momentum or arching the back to lift the bar.
If wrists or forearms fatigue prematurely, reduce weight or shorten range slightly to maintain strict elbow-driven motion and protect joints.
exercises.description: Seated preacher curl using an EZ bar to isolate the biceps by stabilizing the upper arms on an angled bench pad; emphasizes controlled elbow flexion with minimal shoulder involvement. The angled pad reduces cheating and increases time under tension for the biceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl to Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward).

Initiate a controlled dumbbell curl by rotating the palms to face upward and bending the elbows to bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level; keep elbows close to your sides and avoid swinging the torso.

At the top of the curl with dumbbells near shoulder height, rotate palms so they face forward and press the weights overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the shoulders until arms are fully extended.
Pause briefly at full extension with the ribs down and core engaged; avoid overarching the lower back.
Lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled motion to shoulder height, then reverse the curl movement to return the weights to your sides in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the press and inhaling as you lower; maintain a steady tempo and avoid using momentum from the hips or legs.
exercises.description: A standing combination exercise that starts with a dumbbell curl and transitions into an overhead press, targeting the shoulder muscles while also working the arms. Perform the curl to bring weights to shoulder level, then press the dumbbells overhead and control the descent back to the start.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.

Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.

Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.

Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat. Stop 1 rep short of failure (RPE 9). Spinal safety takes priority.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your knees are roughly 90 degrees and your hips are supported while seated (seated position). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your knees are roughly 90 degrees and your hips are supported while seated (seated position).
Select an appropriate weight and ensure the machine's safety catch or lever is engaged before sitting.
Place the balls of both feet shoulder-width on the footplate with toes pointing forward; heels should hang slightly to allow a full range of motion.
Grip the side handles or seat handles to stabilize the torso and unlock the safety if applicable.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet, driving the footplate away by extending your ankles into full plantarflexion (raise your heels as high as possible).
Pause briefly at the top for a strong contraction in the calves, then inhale and slowly lower your heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves (controlled eccentric phase).
Repeat for desired reps. After finishing, lock the safety catch before removing your feet from the footplate.
exercises.description: Seated calf press performed on a lever machine targets the calf muscles by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while seated with knees bent. It isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus complex for focused calf development.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Lever Horizontal Leg Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your knees start bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) without pinching the hip; grip the side handles for stability. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your back fully supported against the pad and feet hip-width on the footplate (mid-foot to whole foot contact).
Adjust the seat so your knees start bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) without pinching the hip; grip the side handles for stability.
Release any safety latch or lock that holds the lever in place before beginning the set.
Inhale, then press the footplate forward by extending your knees and hips until legs are nearly straight; avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Exhale as you drive the plate away, pushing through your heels to emphasize the quads and glutes.
Pause briefly at the top with tension maintained, then slowly bend the knees to return the plate to the start position under control.
Keep your lower back pressed into the pad throughout; do not let the hips lift off the seat or allow the knees to collapse inward.
After completing the set, secure the lever with the safety lock before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated horizontal leg press performed on a lever plate to extend the knees against resistance, primarily targeting the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and calves. The back remains supported on an angled seat while the feet push the footplate forward.

exercises.name: Barbell Straight Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Start standing (upright) with the barbell on the floor or racked at mid-thigh; feet about hip-width apart and toes pointing forward or slightly out. Stop 1 rep short of failure (RPE 9). Spinal safety takes priority.
instructions: Start standing (upright) with the barbell on the floor or racked at mid-thigh; feet about hip-width apart and toes pointing forward or slightly out.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand (pronated) grip and stand tall so the bar hangs at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Set a slight bend in the knees (do not lock them) and brace your core so your spine is neutral; keep the chest up and shoulders back.
Begin the movement by hinging at the hips: push your hips straight back while keeping the bar close to your legs and maintaining the slight knee bend.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid-shin or just below the knees), keeping the back flat and head in a neutral line.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and core—do not let the lower back round.
To return, drive the hips forward forcefully, contract the glutes, and stand tall while keeping the bar close to the body; exhale as you rise.
At the top, avoid hyperextending the lower back—finish with a neutral spine and hips fully closed.
Perform controlled repetitions, focusing on hip hinge mechanics and grip; if grip fails before the hamstrings, use straps or lighter weight.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge barbell lift performed with minimal knee bend to emphasize the hamstrings and posterior chain; the movement lowers the bar by pushing the hips back and returns by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set an appropriate weight on the lever machine and position the ankle/calf cuff or pad behind the working lower leg (just above the heel/ankle). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an appropriate weight on the lever machine and position the ankle/calf cuff or pad behind the working lower leg (just above the heel/ankle).
Stand facing the machine support and lean forward, placing your forearms or chest against the pad/handles so your torso is stable and supported (standing position, body braced).
Place your non-working foot firmly on the floor for support and keep the support-side knee slightly bent to stabilize; the working knee can remain slightly bent as it moves.
Begin with the working thigh roughly in line with your torso (neutral hip). Maintain a neutral spine and avoid arching or rotating the pelvis throughout the movement.
Drive the working leg backward by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is behind the body (do not force hyperextension).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining pelvic stability, then slowly control the lever back to the start position until the hip returns to neutral.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch legs. Keep movement slow and controlled and do not use momentum from the torso or swinging of the leg.
exercises.description: A machine-based single-leg hip extension performed standing with the torso braced on a support; the working leg pushes a lever backward to extend the hip and drive the leg behind the body, emphasizing glute contraction.

exercises.name: Lever Sitting Toe Raise
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the machine bench with your knees bent at about 90 degrees and the padded lever resting across your lower thighs just above the knees.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
Start with your heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet, plantarflexing the ankles to raise your heels as high as possible while keeping the knees stationary.
Hold the top position for a brief pause, squeezing the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping a controlled tempo and maintaining upright torso and stable knee position throughout.
exercises.description: A seated calf-raise performed on a lever-style machine that isolates the lower leg by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while the knees are bent. It emphasizes the soleus and gastrocnemius to build calf strength and size.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and your knees start around a 90° angle when feet are on the footplate; sit upright, feet about shoulder-width apart on the platform. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and your knees start around a 90° angle when feet are on the footplate; sit upright, feet about shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Grip the side handles for stability and unlock the machine's safety latch if present so the sled can move freely.
Brace your core, press through your heels and mid-foot to extend your knees and hips, driving the platform away until legs are almost straight — do not lock the knees.

Pause briefly at the top under control, then inhale and slowly lower the platform by bending the knees until they return to about the starting 90° angle (or slightly deeper) while keeping the back flat against the pad.
Maintain even foot pressure (avoid lifting heels), keep controlled tempo, and breathe out on the pressing (concentric) phase and inhale on the lowering (eccentric) phase.
Rerack or re-engage the safety latch before releasing the handles at the end of the set; stop if you feel your hips rising off the pad or your knees collapsing inward.
exercises.description: Seated leg press on a lever machine where you push a footplate away by extending the hips and knees; primarily develops knee extension strength in the quads while the glutes, hamstrings and calves assist. Performed seated with the back supported and feet placed on the angled platform.

exercises.name: Barbell Glute Bridge (hands on bar)
exercises.notes: Lie supine on the floor with knees bent and feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart; position the loaded barbell across your hips and grasp the bar with both hands on either side to stabilize it. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie supine on the floor with knees bent and feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart; position the loaded barbell across your hips and grasp the bar with both hands on either side to stabilize it.

Brace your core (engage abs) and press your heels into the floor so that your shins are roughly vertical or slightly angled; keep your chin neutral and your upper back and shoulders resting on the floor.

Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips off the floor, extending the hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees (avoid hyperextending the lower back).
Hold the top position briefly and actively squeeze the glutes, keeping the bar stable with your hands and maintaining a neutral spine.
Slowly lower your hips back down under control until your glutes touch or are just above the floor, resetting your core tension as needed.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining even foot placement and steady breathing (exhale on the lift, inhale on the descent).
exercises.description: Supine hip thrust variation with a barbell placed across the hips; drive the hips upward by contracting the glutes until the body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Hands hold the bar at the hips to stabilize the load while the core and posterior chain control the movement.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
Position your shoulders under the padded lever, stand tall and grip the machine handles for stability; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward (or slightly turned in/out to vary emphasis).
Begin with a full stretch: lower your heels down until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves (keep torso upright and spine neutral).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, exhaling and keeping knees straight (a slight knee bend shifts emphasis to the soleus if desired).
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top and squeeze the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the stretched starting position with control—avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and proper alignment throughout.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing calf raise where the shoulders are braced under a padded lever and the heels drop and rise from a platform to load the calf muscles through a full range of motion. Emphasizes plantarflexion to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your upper back and head are supported (supine on the incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level with palms facing forward and elbows bent (upper arms roughly 45° from the torso) while retracting the shoulder blades slightly.
Unrack the dumbbells by pressing them up and then lowering them to start position so the handles are beside your upper chest/shoulder area.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking or hyperextending the elbows); exhale during the press.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the upper chest/shoulder level while inhaling.
Keep a stable core and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; maintain scapular control and smooth, controlled reps for the desired number of repetitions.
If using heavy weights, have a spotter or perform a controlled seated dumbbell rack/unrack to avoid strain when starting or finishing the set.
exercises.description: A pressing movement performed lying supine on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest. Dumbbells allow a slightly greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared with a barbell.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell 2 Point Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your upper back and shoulders supported and one foot planted firmly on the floor near the bench.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your upper back and shoulders supported and one foot planted firmly on the floor near the bench.

Bend the opposite knee and lift that foot off the floor so only one foot is providing support (single‑leg position); press your hips up into a bridge so your torso forms a straight line from shoulder to knee and brace your abs.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand (or a single dumbbell if using one) at chest level with a neutral grip or palms facing forward, elbows bent and tucked at about a 45° angle to your torso.
Inhale, then press the weight straight up by extending your arms until elbows are nearly locked and the dumbbells are over your chest while keeping hips elevated and core tight.
Pause briefly at the top, keeping shoulders stable on the bench and hips high, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to your chest under control while maintaining the bridge position.
Complete the prescribed reps on one leg, then carefully lower your hips, switch legs, and repeat on the other side. Keep movement controlled and avoid hyperextending the lower back.
exercises.description: A bench press performed while holding a hip bridge (single-leg position) to add core and glute stability demand; you press the dumbbells from chest to full arm extension while maintaining an elevated hip. This combines chest pressing with unilateral lower‑body stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.

Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press

Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).

Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).

Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
